title: Indexing Directives, Canonical Graphs, Hreflang, and Structured Data · Runtime Contract: Index Directives
summary: The auditor trusts tags as commands, merges crawl and index policies, accepts malformed JSON-LD, and promises ranking or rich results from syntax. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go index directives increment in cross-signal search metadata report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag by product token, response, precedence, and scope while separating crawling from indexing.
Resolve canonical references and compare redirects, sitemap, internal links, content identity, reachability, and cycles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Competency probe: Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag by product token, response, precedence, and scope while separating crawling from indexing.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Index Directives model · Theory Index Directives, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Disallow and noindex mean the same thing.
